researcher_context.primary_field: Computational biomedical research — lung cancer survival analysis and machine learning for adjuvant chemotherapy recommendations
methodological_interests: Survival analysis and time-to-event modeling (Cox PH, deep survival, accelerated failure time)
Machine learning for treatment effect estimation and individualized treatment recommendation
Causal inference in observational oncology data
LLMs for meta-analysis screening and automated evidence synthesis
adjacent_fields_of_interest: Mathematical oncology and mechanistic tumor models (ODE, PDE, agent-based)
Tumor microenvironment biology — stromal, vascular, and immune compartments
Immuno-oncology, with specific interest in CAR T-cell therapy
Tumor immunology — T-cell exhaustion, antigen escape, neoantigen prediction
Scientific machine learning — physics-informed neural networks, neural ODEs, hybrid mechanistic-ML models
Protein engineering and ML-driven protein design
Self-driving labs, autonomous experimentation, closed-loop active learning
researcher_context.bridge_description: The HIGHEST priority is the triple intersection: statistical/ML approaches in clinical oncology + mechanistic biological models + immunology (CAR T, T-cell biology, tumor-immune dynamics). Also valuable: protein engineering and self-driving labs as method transfer for CAR construct design and active learning in cellular therapy. Prioritise connections that REFRAME how a problem is approached — not connections that simply apply an existing method to a new dataset.
avoid_topics: general LLM benchmarking unrelated to biomedical use
non-cancer dynamical systems with no obvious biomedical relevance
pure application papers — applying existing methods to new datasets without methodological insight
incremental benchmark improvements without new ideas
purely engineering applications of physics-informed ML

quality_philosophy.principle: Quality over quantity. Surface a small number of extremely high-impact, high-quality papers worth deep reading, NOT a quota of mediocre work.
quality_philosophy.shortage_is_acceptable: If fewer than max_total_digest_papers meet the quality bar in a given week, the digest is shorter rather than padded. Explicitly note the shortage in the digest — do NOT lower the bar to fill space.
substance_signals: Introduces a genuinely new method or theoretical framework
Reframes a known problem in a non-obvious way
Reports a surprising empirical finding that contradicts prior work
Bridges two fields in a way no prior paper has
Establishes a new mathematical equivalence between phenomena
weak_signals_to_filter_out: Yet another benchmark of an existing model on a new dataset
Application of a foundation model with no new method
Case studies without a generalisable conclusion
Surveys or reviews without a novel synthesis

digest_caps.foundation_model_inclusion_rule: Include a foundation-model paper ONLY if it introduces a new method, a new biological insight from the model, or a non-obvious reframing — never for benchmarks or applications alone.
